questions.text: Co-packers are not responsible for performing any quality or quantity checks upon receiving components.
The new NEO system allows CSC Supply Planners to manually convert Purchase Requisitions (PRs) to Purchase Orders (POs) but does not provide a mass conversion option.
NEO introduces a standardized, automated process for managing returns from co-packers, improving efficiency and stock management.
Two Purchase Orders are required for direct shipment to a co-packer if a portion of the stock needs to be sent to a Distribution Center first.
In the new NEO system, the conversion from Purchase Requisition to Purchase Order for co-packing will be automated.
The main goal of managing component shipments is to consolidate shipments and optimize logistics.
The Subcontracting Substitution Cockpit allows updating co-packing Purchase Orders based on component stock levels and supersession chains.
The logic for component consumption during co-packing is predefined based on the component's master data.
Level 2 traceability for co-packing only requires tracking batch codes, not quantities.
If the Rounding Profile value in the Customizing Table is blank, the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) defined in the material master is used for rounding.
